topic.title: SAP S/4HANA Integration with SAP TM

learningObjectives.title: Understand ERP-TM integration
learningObjectives.description: Explain how SAP S/4HANA provides transportation demand to SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Understand document synchronization
learningObjectives.description: Describe flow of orders, deliveries, freight units, and execution updates.

learningObjectives.title: Understand master data alignment
learningObjectives.description: Clarify synchronized master data as foundation for planning and execution.

sections.title: SAP S/4HANA Integration with SAP TM

sections.content: SAP S/4HANA is the primary source of business transactions for SAP Transportation Management. Business documents such as Sales Orders, Purchase Orders, Outbound Deliveries and Inbound Deliveries are transferred automatically from SAP S/4HANA to SAP TM, where transportation planning and execution take place.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP S/4HANA creates transportation demand, while SAP TM plans, executes and settles transportation.

A customer places a Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA. After the Outbound Delivery is created, the delivery is automatically transferred to SAP TM. SAP TM builds Freight Units, optimizes transportation, creates Freight Orders and sends execution updates back to SAP S/4HANA after shipment completion.

sections.title: Responsibilities of SAP S/4HANA vs SAP TM
Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain master data in SAP S/4HANA as the system of record and synchronize it with SAP TM to ensure consistent planning and execution.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An automotive manufacturer integrated SAP S/4HANA with SAP TM to automatically transfer more than 25,000 outbound deliveries daily. Transportation planning became fully automated, reducing manual planning effort by over 80%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: A very common SAP TM certification question asks: Which system creates Sales Orders and Deliveries? Answer: SAP S/4HANA. Which system creates Freight Units and Freight Orders? Answer: SAP TM.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: SAP TM does not replace SAP S/4HANA ERP. ERP manages business transactions, while SAP TM specializes in transportation planning, execution and settlement.

items: SAP S/4HANA is the source of transportation demand.
SAP TM receives deliveries and creates Freight Units.
Master data is synchronized between both systems.
Execution updates and settlement integrate back with SAP S/4HANA.
SAP S/4HANA and SAP TM together provide end-to-end logistics management.
